5e992426223d.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
5e992426223d.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
5e992426223d.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

f8f4af25c921.title: Build review volume — you lack statistical credibility
f8f4af25c921.description: Only 11 Google reviews. A single bad review moves your average visibly. Encourage satisfied customers to leave reviews.

bc7fa40b9db5.title: Close the hygiene gap — 3 to 5
bc7fa40b9db5.description: An FSA rating of 3 is visible on your Google listing and the FSA website. Customers see it before they see your menu. Address the inspector's specific findings.
bc7fa40b9db5.expected_upside: Remove the trust ceiling on your overall score

e43db0c0f299.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
e43db0c0f299.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

cc3a13672183.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
cc3a13672183.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
cc3a13672183.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

3f3236c50702.title: Strong rating masks weak competitive position
3f3236c50702.description: Your 4.8/5 Google rating feels dominant but you sit below the local median (P0.0). Peer venues achieve similar or better ratings — in this market, 4.5 is table stakes, not a differentiator. Don't mistake the number for an advantage.
3f3236c50702.management_implication: The rating looks good in isolation but not in context.

19fca447f549.title: Ranked #3 of 3 locally — peers are ahead
19fca447f549.description: At percentile 0.0, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

1572742c3180.title: Guest experience trailing — investigate what peers do differently
1572742c3180.description: You trail peers by 1.3 points on experience. This isn't a label problem — it means customers are getting a measurably better experience on this dimension from your competitors.

fd0d04d12b3e.title: Compliance record weakest in peer set — creates inspection exposure
fd0d04d12b3e.description: You trail peers by 3.2 points on trust. This isn't a label problem — it means customers are getting a measurably better experience on this dimension from your competitors.

70dea5fa57fe.title: Harder to book/find than competitors — friction losing customers
70dea5fa57fe.description: You trail peers by 2.0 points on conversion. This isn't a label problem — it means customers are getting a measurably better experience on this dimension from your competitors.
